# ane-tts

> Accelerating TTS inference on Apple Silicon by leveraging the Neural Engine. Targeting Fish S2 Pro (5B) on consumer Macs.

## Problem
Fish S2 Pro (5B params, best open-source TTS) runs at 0.69x RTF on Metal GPU via MLX. Sub-realtime. The ANE (15.8 TOPS) sits idle during inference.

## Approach
Two backends: direct ANE access (maderix/ANE) and CoreML (stable, for everyone).
Pipeline parallelism: slow AR on GPU + fast AR on ANE concurrently.
ANEMLL for ANE-optimized CoreML conversion with KV cache.

## Status
Research phase COMPLETE (14 experiments on M2 Max). Engineering phase ACTIVE.

### What is proven
- Research-phase timing data (Experiments 0.2-14): pipeline profiling, CoreML vs MLX throughput, ANE characterization, Swift GCD overlap — valid, weight-independent measurements
- CoreML direct conversion (no KV cache): 0.84x RTF measured (Experiment 14)
- CoreML is 1.46x faster than MLX for slow AR (23.8ms vs 34.7ms per token)
- Swift GCD gives 45-51% GPU+ANE overlap for pipeline parallelism
- Weight adapter correctness: 398 tensors verified, embedding parity (max_err=0.0), single-layer parity (max_err < 0.001), full-model one-step parity (max_err=0.000038, top-1/top-5 match)

### What is NOT proven (pending re-conversion and re-benchmark)
- KV-cache multi-step parity
- ANEMLL-converted model performance (previous benchmarks INVALID — ran on broken weights)
- Audio output quality
- Any RTF claim beyond the 0.84x CoreML-direct measurement
- Previous "45ms" and "1.03x RTF" numbers are INVALID

### ANEMLL conversion
Previous conversion completed structurally but used a broken weight adapter (3 bugs: missing embeddings, dropped QK norms, broken QKV split). Re-conversion required on corrected weights.

## Key Tools
- maderix/ANE (github.com/maderix/ANE) — direct ANE access, 6.2K stars
- ANEMLL (github.com/Anemll/Anemll) — LLMs on ANE
- coremltools — Apple's official CoreML conversion
- mlx-audio — current TTS inference (Metal GPU)

## Models
- Fish S2 Pro 5B BF16 (~10GB) — target model, best quality
- Qwen3-TTS 1.7B BF16 (~4GB) — potential draft model
- Qwen3-TTS 0.6B 4-bit (~1GB) — small draft model for ANE

Decision tree at docs/DECISION_TREE.md. Lab notebook at docs/LAB_NOTEBOOK.md.
